reasoning: On March 8, 2025, the head of Myanmar's military junta, Min Aung Hlaing, officially announced that the planned national election will be held in either December 2025 or January 2026. This announcement effectively rules out any possibility of an election being held before the question's deadline of July 1, 2025. Given the active civil war and historical delays, earlier surprise elections are virtually impossible. Therefore, the probability of an election occurring before July 1, 2025, is minimal.
